updates.title: Heat wave & irrigation timing
updates.brief: Hot, windy stretch ahead — water ahead of it, not after. Full notes:
body: We've got several days of 95°+ and wind in the forecast. The mistake to avoid this week is waiting until the crop looks stressed to start a pass — by then you're already behind and playing catch-up in the worst conditions.
Crop water use climbs with wind as much as heat. A 90° day with a 20 mph wind pulls more out of the canopy than a calm 95° day, so don't let a cooler-looking forecast talk you into backing off.

If a field is anywhere close to its refill point right now, start it before the wave hits so the profile is full going in. It's a lot easier to hold a full tank than to refill one in the middle of a hot, windy week.
Probes are the tiebreaker. If the bottom sensor is still moving but the top has gone flat, you're drying out up top — that field wants water even if the daily totals look okay.
